translations.titulo: Early Diagnosis and Management of Osteoarthritis
translations.descricao: Osteoarthritis (OA) is the most prevalent degenerative joint disease, characterized by joint pain and stiffness, mainly affecting knees, hips, and hands. Early diagnosis is essential for non-pharmacological and pharmacological interventions that improve quality of life.
translations.sus.indicacao: Diagnosis indicated for adults with persistent joint pain >3 months, age >50 years or risk factors such as obesity and previous trauma. There is no routine population screening, but evaluation in primary care for symptoms.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Adults >50 years with joint symptoms or risk factors (BMI >30 kg/m², high-impact occupations).
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: The Brazilian Society of Rheumatology recommends clinical diagnosis for patients with chronic joint pain and functional limitation. Evaluation in at-risk individuals for early detection.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: The global prevalence of symptomatic OA is approximately 7% in adults >50 years, reaching 30% in >65 years. In Brazil, it is estimated at 6.2% in the adult population, with greater impact on women.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: The annual incidence is 10-20 cases per 1,000 people >45 years globally. In Brazil, about 1.5% per year in the elderly.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Direct mortality is low (<1%), but OA contributes to 2-3% of deaths due to disability and cardiovascular comorbidities. In Brazil, it is associated with 1.2% of hospitalizations for NCDs.
